title: Pet Urine on Wool Carpet
timeEstimate: Pro-lane immediately; cold blot only at home
stainChemistry: Pet urine on wool carpet stacks the worst of both worlds: urine crystals reactivate with humidity AND wool felts under hot water plus agitation plus alkaline cleaners. The standard pet-urine fix on synthetic carpet — saturate with retail enzyme cleaner — is BANNED on wool because the broad-spectrum enzymes in those products would damage the wool fiber itself. Hot water sets the urine AND felts the wool. Alkaline cleaners (OxiClean, ammonia) felt wool AND lock urine deeper. Chlorine bleach destroys wool. The pad below the wool can also become saturated, and wool is more vulnerable than synthetic carpet at every step. Pro chemistry uses a specialty wool-safe enzyme (one that targets urine but leaves wool alone) at controlled dilution plus cold extraction. Home protocol is blot what you can with cold water, then pro IMMEDIATELY.
whyThisWorks: Cold press-blot extracts bulk urine before crystals lock in. A wool-carpet specialist can then use a wool-safe targeted enzyme plus cold sub-surface extraction to clear what's left without felting or damaging the fiber. Home cannot match this: every retail pet-urine product contains broad-spectrum enzymes that damage wool, and any aggressive home extraction risks felting. Cold blot is the safe home action; everything else needs pro chemistry.
scienceNote: Mechanism (pro-facing): pet urine is urea + uric acid crystals + salts + protein; uric acid is hydrophobic and reactivates with humidity. Wool is keratin protein. Pro chemistry uses urease-only enzyme (NOT protease — protease digests wool keratin) at controlled dilution plus cold acetic acid plus cold sub-surface extraction.
defaultAssumption: Wool carpet + pet urine = pro IMMEDIATELY. Home protocol: gloves + cold press-blot + pro within hours. UV blacklight to find full extent (urine spreads farther than visible). For Oriental wool rugs, even fresher action.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Gloves on. UV blacklight in dark room — urine glows yellow-green. Tape outer edge with painter's tape. Pet urine spreads farther than visible — treating too small means recurrence.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Cold water, white cloth, dry towel + weight
spottingProtocol.instruction: Press a cloth dampened with COLD water on the spot. Vertical pressure only — no rubbing. Cold dilutes uric acid + prevents protein setting. Weighted dry towel 5 min after to extract.
Cold NSD sponge press. Suspends fresh sweat-and-protein component without enzymes. NEVER pour on wool — flood + felt risk.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Pro wool-safe urine-targeted enzyme enzyme product
spottingProtocol.instruction: Pro applies urine-targeted enzyme. Strict application without flooding. Cold sub-surface extraction follows.
Diluted acetic acid neutralizes residual ammonia. STRICT 60-sec ceiling. Flush cold immediately.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Dry towels + weight, fan, UV blacklight
spottingProtocol.instruction: Weighted dry towels overnight. Fan-dry. Re-blacklight verify all areas treated. Persistent UV signal = pad contamination, sub-surface extraction next.

steps: 1. Gloves + UV blacklight + tape edges of contaminated area.
3. Cold wool-safe NSD sponge press for fresh urine.
5. Acetic acid 60-sec spotter for ammonia neutralization.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold throughout — wool felts above 30°C when wet
warnings: NEVER protein-digesting enzymes — digest wool keratin
NEVER hot water / steam — felts wool + sets protein
NEVER alkaline (OxiClean / ammonia) — felts wool + locks urine
NEVER agitation / scrubbing — felts wool on contact
NEVER pour solution on wool carpet — flood = felt
Pad contamination on wool carpet = pad replacement, not extraction

homeSolutions.agent: Honest Answer — Pro IMMEDIATELY for Wool Carpet
homeSolutions.instruction: Pet urine on wool carpet is the worst combination in the pet-urine family. Wool's banned tools (no enzymes, no hot water, no alkaline, no agitation) eliminate everything that lifts pet urine from synthetic carpet. Retail pet enzyme cleaners contain protein-digesting enzyme which DIGESTS WOOL. Home protocol = gloves + cold press-blot + pro IMMEDIATELY. Wool-safe pet-urine chemistry exists but is pro-only.

Disposable gloves (biohazard). If you have a UV blacklight (cheap on Amazon, ~$15), turn off the lights and scan the area — pet urine glows yellow-green, often spreading WAY farther than the visible spot. Mark the full edge with painter's tape. Treating too small = the dog re-marks the area or odor returns.

Press a clean white cloth dampened with COLD water onto the spot. Vertical pressure only — never rub or scrub. Cold dilutes the uric acid + prevents protein setting. Lift, refresh cloth, press again until cloth comes up clean. Don't pour water on wool carpet — flood + agitation = felt.
Stack dry paper towels on the spot, weight with a heavy book, leave 30 minutes. Wool carpet retains moisture deep — extract aggressively (without rubbing) before pro arrives.

Don't wait. Don't apply Nature's Miracle or any retail pet enzyme — they contain protein-digesting enzyme that digests wool. Tell the pro: "Pet urine on wool carpet, [cat / dog], age [hours / overnight], gloves + cold press-blot ONLY at home. NO enzyme cleaner, NO hot water, NO OxiClean, NO household pet products applied. Need wool-safe urine-targeted enzyme + cold sub-surface extraction."

steps: 1. Wool carpet + pet urine = pro IMMEDIATELY.
2. Gloves on. UV blacklight to find full extent.
3. NO retail pet enzyme cleaners — they contain protein-digesting enzyme that digests wool.
4. Cold damp-cloth press-blot — vertical pressure, no rubbing.
5. Weighted dry towels 30 min for extraction.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop after cold press-blot. Any retail pet enzyme cleaner on wool carpet digests the wool — don't apply.

NEVER scrubbing / agitation wet wool carpet

protein-digesting enzymes digest wool keratin equally with sweat protein
urine-targeted enzymes are wool-safe but pro-only

escalation.when: Always — wool carpet + pet urine is pro-only IMMEDIATELY. Pad contamination, recurring spots, or any retail enzyme already applied = even more urgent.
escalation.whatToTell: "Pet urine on wool carpet, [cat / dog], age [hours / overnight], gloves + cold press-blot ONLY at home. NO enzyme cleaner, NO hot water, NO OxiClean, NO household pet products applied. Need wool-safe urine-targeted enzyme + cold sub-surface extraction. UV blacklight extent: [size]."

contextQuestions: Has anyone applied retail pet enzyme (Nature's Miracle / Rocco & Roxie)?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Cat urine harder than dog (felinine + mercaptans). Recurring same-spot accidents = pad almost certainly contaminated. Oriental wool rugs need specialist with hand-knot expertise. Pre-applied retail pet enzyme = wool likely partially digested where applied; disclose honestly.
